message:
perf(render): wire set_fast into Frame and Buffer drawing hot paths (bd-3ons3)

Buffer::set_fast() existed with safe fallback to set() for non-trivial
cases (wide chars, continuations, opacity, nested scissors), but was
only used in benchmarks. Replace set() with set_fast() in the hot
rendering paths:

- Frame::print_text_clipped (main widget text rendering path)
- Buffer::draw_horizontal_line / draw_vertical_line
- Buffer::print_text_clipped + filler cells
- Buffer::draw_border (edges + corners)

For the common case (opaque single-width ASCII), this skips scissor
intersection, opacity blending, and overlap cleanup. Falls through
to full set() automatically when needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
